title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Twain, Mark
summary: Huck Finn narrates his flight from civilization and his adventures on the Mississippi River. After being taken in by the Widow Douglas and Miss Watson, Huck returns to rags, reunites with Tom Sawyer, and fakes his death to escape his abusive father. Huck meets Jim, a runaway enslaved man, and they travel on a raft, facing conmen, feuding families, wrecks, and moral dilemmas. Huck matures, questions social mores and slavery, and protects Jim. In the end Jim gains freedom, Tom is wounded but recovers, and Huck faces choices about society and independence and prepares to seek new adventures downriver westward.

characters.name: Huckleberry Finn
characters.personality: practical, independent, skeptical of adult hypocrisy; morally developing
characters.lifespan: survives; ends planning further adventures

characters.name: Tom Sawyer
characters.personality: romantic, adventurous, theatrical, obsessed with elaborate schemes
characters.lifespan: wounded late in the story but survives; identity clarified at end

characters.name: Jim
characters.personality: loyal, caring, superstitious, sensible
characters.lifespan: survives and is freed by the story's end

characters.name: The Widow Douglas
characters.personality: pious, kindly, intent on civilizing Huck
characters.lifespan: remains Huck's guardian figure through much of the story

characters.name: Miss Watson
characters.personality: religious, stern, hypocritical in parts
characters.lifespan: dies during the course of events (her death affects Jim's status)

characters.name: Pap Finn (Huck's father)
characters.personality: violent, alcoholic, abusive, selfish
characters.lifespan: disappears/found dead during the narrative

characters.name: Judge Thatcher
characters.personality: responsible, prudent, guardian of Huck's fortune
characters.lifespan: survives; acts as trustee for Huck's money

characters.name: Aunt Polly
characters.personality: strict but affectionate; Tom's aunt who enforces discipline
characters.lifespan: appears at the end and survives

characters.name: Mary (Tom's cousin)
characters.personality: straightforward, part of Tom's domestic circle
characters.lifespan: appears in Tom-related scenes; survives

characters.name: Joe Harper
characters.personality: Tom's friend and fellow adventurer
characters.lifespan: minor character; survives

characters.name: Ben Rogers
characters.personality: member of Tom's circle
characters.lifespan: minor character; survives

characters.name: The Duke (of Bridgewater)
characters.personality: confidence man, theatrical, unscrupulous
characters.lifespan: exposed as a fraud and run out of towns by the end of episodes

characters.name: The King (the 'Dauphin')
characters.personality: fraudulent, cruel in schemes, greedy
characters.lifespan: exposed and driven off; ends humiliated

characters.name: Colonel Grangerford
characters.personality: genteel, proud, involved in a violent family feud
characters.lifespan: killed in the Grangerford–Shepherdson feud

characters.name: Buck Grangerford
characters.personality: friendly, naive, befriends Huck
characters.lifespan: killed in the family feud

characters.name: Sophia Grangerford
characters.personality: romantic, elopes
characters.lifespan: elopes with Harney Shepherdson; survives

characters.name: Harney Shepherdson
characters.personality: romantic rival of the feud, elopes with Sophia
characters.lifespan: survives; elopes successfully

characters.name: Emmeline Grangerford
characters.personality: sentimental young poet (her verses read in the house)
characters.lifespan: already dead when Huck sees her poems

characters.name: Stephen Dowling Bots
characters.personality: poetical, sentimental, ridiculous in his verse
characters.lifespan: minor; his verses are mocked but he survives within episodes

characters.name: Old Hank Bunker
characters.personality: local character encountered in adventures
characters.lifespan: minor; survives the episode

characters.name: Col. (or Colonel) Sherburn
characters.personality: proud, delivers a famous speech against lynch mobs after killing Boggs
characters.lifespan: survives; remains unlynched after confronting mob

characters.name: Colonel (or Old) Boggs
characters.personality: drunken, talkative town character
characters.lifespan: shot and killed (by Sherburn)

characters.name: Peter Wilks
characters.personality: respected dead man whose estate becomes central to a swindle
characters.lifespan: dead at his funeral (central to the Wilks episode)

characters.name: William and Harvey (the Wilks brothers)
characters.personality: relations of Peter Wilks; targets of imposture by the Duke and King
characters.lifespan: their identities and estate are subjects of the con; survive within the narrative

characters.name: Mary Jane Wilks
characters.personality: kind, trusting, morally upright
characters.lifespan: survives; shown pity and trust by Huck

characters.name: Uncle Silas Phelps
characters.personality: stolid, hospitable; owner of the farm where Tom is treated as nephew
characters.lifespan: survives; involved in final rescue scenes

characters.name: Aunt Sally Phelps
characters.personality: hospitable, conventional, easily flustered
characters.lifespan: survives; central to the final chapters

characters.name: Adolphus (a town character)
characters.personality: eccentric town figure (appears in comic episodes)
characters.lifespan: minor; survives

locations.name: Mississippi River (general, scenes on the river and raft)
Missouri (state; setting region of many scenes)
Hannibal / 'St. Petersburg' (fictional town based on Hannibal, Missouri)
Cairo (referred to in "Running by Cairo"), Illinois (river town)
Arkansaw (Arkansas; the farm in Arkansaw referred to in the book)
Pike County (dialect reference; Pike County, Missouri)
